sourceScope.primarySourceFile: Practice Notes/AAA - C3 Phase 2 - Sep 2006 - life_c3.8.pdf
sourceScope.sourceReference: American Academy of Actuaries practice note, September 2006
sourceScope.sourceStatusNote: Companion guidance / implementation note; not authoritative regulatory text.

observedSectionWindows.note: Front matter, products covered, and common practice guidance.
VA RBC vs VACARVM differences, model granularity, and starting assets.
Scenario generation, calibration, fund modeling, and economic assumptions.
Prudent best estimate, discount rate, and core assumption setting.
Revenue sharing, negative assets, interpolation, and informed projection.
Alternative Methodology and Standard Scenario opener.
Standard Scenario tail, reinsurance, and hedging entry point.
C-3 Phase I consistency, certification, and documentation requirements.
Peer review, glossary terms, appendix Q&A, and closing boundary.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single practice note file.
Do not widen into the other Practice Notes files in the same folder.
Keep VM-20, VM-21, VM-22, VM-30, VM-31, AG files, and NY regulation files review-only unless the same batch intentionally includes the referenced operational text.
Keep the disclaimer and the non-binding status visible as companion-guidance context, not as authoritative regulatory text.
Use page locators as the primary citation anchor; line references are not expected for this PDF slice.
Keep a page-image wording backstop visible because the text layer is noisy in places and should not be overclaimed.
exclusions: No full-document processing outside this source file.
No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other Practice Notes files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Front matter, introduction, products covered, and common practice guidance

topicMap.label: Scenario generation, calibration, fund modeling, and economic assumptions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the scenario-generation slice separate from the assumption-setting and methodology sections.

topicMap.label: Prudent best estimate, discount rate, and core assumption setting
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the assumption framework narrow before the revenue-sharing and interpolation material begins.

topicMap.label: Revenue-sharing closeout, interpolation, and informed projection details
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the valuation-date bridge and interpolation mechanics together before Section 8 begins.

topicMap.label: Alternative Methodology and Standard Scenario opener
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the AM / Standard Scenario relationship readable and do not widen into the hedge tail.

topicMap.label: Standard Scenario tail, reinsurance, and hedging entry point
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the reinsurance and hedge-credit mechanics contiguous and stop before the certification layer.

topicMap.label: Certification, documentation, and peer review interfaces
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep certification and documentation together while the reporting/control layer is in view.

topicMap.label: Peer review, glossary terms, appendix Q&A, and closing boundary
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the appendix / closeout separate so the source ends cleanly at page 93.
